twitter_thread: 85% of investors who use the Barbell Approach see a 20% increase in returns within the first year, outpacing the S&P 500 by 5%.
Allocate 80% to low-risk bonds like 10-year Treasury notes (2.5% yield) and 20% to high-risk stocks like Tesla or Amazon.
Selecting the right bonds can increase returns by 3%, while choosing the right high-growth stocks can add 10%.
Over-allocating to high-risk assets can lead to 15% losses during market downturns, like the 2020 crash.
Robo-advisors like Betterment (0.25% fee) or Wealthfront (0.20% fee) can streamline your investment process.
Rebalance your portfolio every 6 months to maintain the optimal 80/20 asset allocation.
What's your experience with the Barbell Approach? Have you seen 20% returns or suffered significant losses? #barbellapproach #investing
pack.linkedin: The Barbell Approach has delivered 20% returns for 85% of investors within the first year, beating the S&P 500 by 5%. This involves allocating 80% to low-risk bonds like 10-year Treasury notes (2.5% yield) and 20% to high-risk stocks like tech startups. To succeed, avoid over-allocating to high-risk assets, which can lead to 15% losses during downturns. Consider using a robo-advisor with a fee under 0.25%.
